# School Stack

## The complete operating system for microschools.

---

## What We Are

School Stack is a software company that builds and sells school management platforms to microschool and small private school owners. Every feature in the platform was built inside a real, operating microschool — Sage Field in Texas — and proven there before being offered to anyone else.

The microschool market is one of the fastest-growing segments in American education: $2.7 billion in 2024, projected to reach $9.6 billion by 2033. Between 750,000 and 2.1 million students are currently enrolled in microschools nationwide. The software serving them is fragmented, outdated, or built for the wrong audience entirely.

School Stack is the only platform in this market that covers the full operational journey — from the first time a parent sees your school's website, through their application, their signed contract, their first tuition payment, and into daily classroom operations — in a single unified system, built specifically for how microschools actually work.

---

## The Problem We Solve

Microschool founders are brilliant operators who built something special. But they're running their schools across five to ten disconnected tools — Google Forms for enrollment, Venmo for payments, Gmail threads for parent communication, PDFs for contracts, Wix for a website, Excel for budget. Every tool is a context switch. Every handoff between tools is a chance for something to fall through.

The software alternatives that exist are either built for large school districts (expensive, overwhelming, wrong assumptions), built for childcare and daycare (Brightwheel, Kangarootime), or too lightweight to run a real school operation (Spark, Omella). Nothing was designed for the microschool operator managing 15–80 students, running every department alone or with a tiny team, and needing software that works like a natural extension of how they already think about their school.

That is the gap School Stack owns.

---

## What We Sell

A fully branded, white-labeled school management platform — configured with each customer's school name, domain, colors, and programs. Not a template. Not a DIY builder. A complete system handed to them ready to use, with concierge onboarding.

Customers choose a Stack based on their school's size and needs, then add custom features on top whenever they need something specific.

---

## The Four Stacks

### Starter Stack — For solo founders just launching

_1–2 teachers, up to 20 students, $79/month_

Everything needed to open a school and start enrolling families:

- Full branded school website (homepage, program pages, team, FAQ, philosophy, contact)
- Multi-step enrollment application with health info, emergency contacts, and digital signatures
- Basic admin portal (applications, students, parents, transactions)
- Parent portal (dashboard, forms, billing)
- Stripe payments for registration fees and tuition
- Enrollment confirmation emails

---

### Growth Stack — For schools gaining traction

_3–5 teachers, 20–60 students, $149/month_

Everything in Starter, plus the tools to manage a real team and a real pipeline:

- Teacher portal (student dashboard, categorized notes, hours tracking, staff feed, messages)
- Leads CRM (waitlist + contact form submissions, pipeline statuses, tags, call notes, CSV export)
- Digital contracts (rich text editor, version history, digital signatures, parent and staff signature blocks)
- School calendar (events, recurrence, RSVP, email reminders, program-specific sharing)
- Marketing pages (tour booking with live calendar, info session RSVP, open house tracking)
- Admin messaging and full email audit log

---

### Academy Stack — For established schools with a full team

_Full team, 60–100 students, $249/month_

Everything in Growth, plus financial infrastructure and operational depth:

- Budget and finance module (revenue, expenses, receipt uploads, monthly summaries)
- Mercury bank sync for automatic transaction imports
- Pending payment requests (admin creates, parent sees in billing)
- Advanced reporting (enrollment pipeline counts, student rosters by program and teacher)
- Real-time Discord admin notifications (payments, form submissions, errors)
- Parent portal impersonation (admin views any parent's dashboard for troubleshooting)
- Competitor analysis dashboard

---

### Custom Stack — Built to order, on top of any baseline

_100+ students or specific needs — contact for pricing_

Some schools need something specific that doesn't fit a tier. We scope it, price it, and build it:

- Custom enrollment flows for unique program structures
- SMS integrations
- Attendance tracking systems
- Google Calendar sync
- Custom tuition structures and payment plans
- Multi-location support
- Custom data exports and reports
- Volunteer management
- Learning plan tools
- Any workflow the school needs

Annual plans available at 2 months free. 14-day free trial on all tiers.

---

## The Full Platform — Every Feature

### Public Website & Marketing

A complete, purpose-built school website — not Wix, not a template, every section designed for school marketing:

- Homepage with hero, programs overview, educational philosophy, team bios, searchable FAQ, enrollment CTA, donations, social media, and contact
- Dedicated pages per program with curriculum breakdowns, image galleries, and daily schedules
- About, Our Story, Vision, and Educational Philosophy pages
- Tuition and pricing page, academic calendar, privacy policy, terms of service
- Donate page with fund allocation breakdown and Stripe integration

### Lead Capture

Every touchpoint to capture a prospective family before they leave:

- Waitlist dialog (accessible from any page — parent, child, program interest)
- Contact dialog (name, email, phone, subject, message)
- Info session RSVP form (parent info, multiple children, program interest, referral source, questions)
- Tour booking wizard (3-step: live calendar + time slot → family info → confirmation)
- Floating SMS button and enrollment announcement popup

### Enrollment System

Two application pathways to match different family comfort levels:

Full application (5 steps): child info, parent/guardian info, health and medical info, emergency contacts, signatures and agreements — with partial save and resume so families don't lose progress.

Quick apply (4 steps): abbreviated version for lower-friction signups.

Both feed into the same admin pipeline. Enrollment codes (discount/promo) are supported. Parents track their application status from their own dashboard.

### Admin Portal — 16 modules

- **Dashboard** — Program start date countdowns, at-a-glance overview
- **Applications** — Kanban pipeline + table view, approve/deny with notes, health form uploads (PDF/JPEG/PNG/WEBP/HEIC), enrollment progress checklist, triggered emails (confirmation, reminder, info session invite)
- **Students** — Full records (personal, health, learning profile, behavioral strategies, parent relationships), teacher assignments, soft delete with data preservation
- **Parents** — Account overview, contact info, custody status, associated children and application history
- **Programs** — Students organized by program and teacher with classroom groupings and counts
- **Transactions** — All Stripe payments tracked with fee breakdown, revenue exclusion toggles, linked navigation to related student/parent/application
- **Leads CRM** — Unified waitlist + contact form pipeline, manual lead creation, statuses, predefined tag system, call notes, CSV export, active/inactive tabs
- **Budget & Finance** — Revenue tracking, expenses with receipt uploads, income by source, monthly summaries
- **Calendar** — School events with recurrence, RSVP, email + in-app reminders, program-specific sharing, attachment links
- **Messages** — Internal staff messaging with threading
- **Emails** — Full audit log of every email sent, with content viewing and attachment download
- **Marketing** — Tour bookings (pending/confirmed/cancelled/completed/no-show), open house RSVPs, info session RSVPs, tour blackout date management, lead-to-enrollment conversion tracking
- **Contracts** — Rich text editor, custom form fields (text, yes/no, dropdown, required markers), digital signature blocks for parent and staff, version history with side-by-side diff viewer
- **Competitor Analysis** — Per-school profiles, feature comparisons, strengths/weaknesses assessment, social media tracking
- **Property Planning** — School improvement planning with before/after visuals and goal tracking
- **Impersonate** — Admin views any parent's portal for troubleshooting, without needing their login

### Parent Portal

Everything a parent needs, in one place:

- Dashboard with enrollment checklist, form completion status, assigned teachers, signature status, payment status
- Billing with payment history, pending requests by program, full Stripe transaction detail
- Forms: immunization records, health forms, health statements, digital contract signing, photo release consent (Full/Limited/None), medication plans, authorized pickup persons
- Calendar with event details and RSVP
- Real-time messages with school staff
- Emergency contacts management
- Profile with image upload
- Volunteer interest submission (skills, availability, areas)

### Teacher Portal

- Dashboard with assigned students by program and classroom
- Student notes (categorized: general, behavioral, academic, social, health — shareable or private, with file/image attachments)
- Hours tracking by student and program
- Calendar with full event details
- Real-time messages with parents and admin
- Staff feed (post updates, mention colleagues, share information)

### Payments & Financial Infrastructure

- Stripe checkout for: registration fees, summer tuition (weekly or full plan), school year tuition, aftercare, homeschool drop-in, donations, combined multi-item checkout
- Stripe webhooks: payment confirmation triggers transaction record, confirmation email, Discord alert, fee calculations
- Mercury bank sync
- Pending payment requests (admin creates per student/program, parent sees in billing)
- Enrollment code redemption (promo and discount codes)
- Revenue exclusion flags (mark donations/refunds as excluded from revenue totals)
- Multi-child combined registration checkout

### Authentication & Roles

- Email/password, magic link (OTP), Google OAuth
- Password reset
- Three roles: parent, teacher, admin — each with protected portals and role-based redirects

### Notifications & Communication

- Transactional emails: enrollment confirmation, reminder, info session invite, open house reminder, payment confirmation per payment type
- Real-time Discord admin notifications on payments, errors, and form submissions
- In-app calendar reminders
- SMS contact button on public site

### File Storage & Document Management

- Student health forms (multi-file, preview, download, delete — HEIC conversion supported)
- Immunization records (parent and admin upload paths)
- Religious exemption documents
- Expense receipts
- Profile images (parent and student)
- Teacher note attachments
- All stored in Supabase Storage with signed URLs

### Data & Reporting

- Applications pipeline (status counts, by program)
- Student roster by program and teacher
- Lead pipeline with status/tag counts and CSV export
- Transaction history with fee breakdown and revenue totals
- Budget monthly summaries
- Email send audit log
- Tour/RSVP conversion tracking
- Attendance check-in/check-out records

---

## What School Stack Replaces

| What schools use today                 | School Stack replaces it with                                                   |
| -------------------------------------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| Google Forms for enrollment            | Multi-step application portal with health info, contracts, and parent dashboard |
| Venmo or manual invoicing              | Stripe checkout with full transaction history, payment types, and fee tracking  |
| Email chains with parents              | Parent portal with forms, messages, billing, and calendar                       |
| PDFs for contracts                     | Digital contracts with version history, custom fields, and digital signatures   |
| Sticky notes or spreadsheets for leads | Built-in CRM with pipeline, tags, call notes, and CSV export                    |
| Wix or Squarespace for the website     | Purpose-built school marketing site with enrollment-specific pages              |
| Google Docs for teacher notes          | Teacher portal with categorized, shareable notes and file attachments           |
| Excel for budget                       | Budget module with receipts, income tracking, and monthly reports               |

---

## Competitive Landscape

### Who exists and why they fall short

**SchoolCues** ($1/student/month, ~$75/mo min) is the price-value incumbent — broad modules, affordable, 10+ years in the market. But its billing is confusing and hard to navigate, the UI is clunky, there is no CRM or admissions pipeline, no teacher observation notes, no health profiles, no contract versioning, and no budget tracking. Customers love the support team but frequently cite the software itself as frustrating.

**Spark School Software** ($34.99/mo) was built "by microschoolers for microschoolers" and has the right identity — but very little depth. No CRM, no health forms, no contracts, no application pipeline, no tour booking, no budget module, no behavioral notes. Works for schools that need just a basic gradebook and payment link.

**Brightwheel** ($600M+ valuation) is the category giant in childcare/daycare — exceptional parent UX, strong brand, viral word-of-mouth. But it is a childcare product, not a school product. No academic SIS, no enrollment wizard, no learning profiles, no CRM, no contracts. The lesson to take from Brightwheel: exceptional parent UX drives viral adoption. That is a design principle built into School Stack from day one.

**Omella** (free + $1.99/transaction) handles payments and forms for microschools at zero monthly cost — strong hook for new founders. But it is not a school management system. No parent portal, no teacher dashboard, no CRM, no applications pipeline. Founders who start with Omella outgrow it quickly. That is a direct acquisition opportunity for School Stack.

**PowerSchool, MySchoolWorx, Kangarootime** are all built for the wrong audience — large districts, faith-based K-12, or childcare centers. Wrong scale, wrong assumptions, wrong price.

### The gap nobody fills

Every competitor fails at the same things: a unified CRM from first inquiry to enrolled student, a multi-step application wizard with health forms and waivers, contract versioning with digital signatures, student health and behavioral profiles, teacher observation notes with attachments, budget tracking, multi-type Stripe checkout, and real-time operational notifications.

**School Stack already has all of these — in production, proven at a real school.**

### Our positioning

School Stack occupies the position no competitor holds: high microschool specificity combined with enterprise-grade operational depth. The question we ask every prospect is not "does your current software have a gradebook?" — it is "does your current software handle everything from the first inquiry to the signed contract to the first tuition payment in one unified system, with zero data re-entry?"

---

## Why It's Different

**Built by a founder, for founders.** School Stack was not designed by a software company guessing at school needs. It was built by someone who ran Sage Field from 0 to 30 students using it. Every feature exists because a real microschool needed it. That is a differentiation that money cannot buy and cannot be copied.

**The full customer journey in one system.** The public marketing site, tour booking, waitlist, application, contract, tuition, daily feed, and messaging are all one product. No other platform in this price range manages the experience from "stranger on the internet sees your school" through "enrolled student in the classroom" without leaving the system.

**Operational depth at small school pricing.** Enterprise features — contract versioning with diff viewer, multi-type Stripe checkout with webhook automation, student health profiles, behavioral strategies, CRM with tags and call logs, budget module with bank sync — at $79–$249/month.

**Concierge setup.** Every customer gets their instance configured with their school's name, domain, programs, and branding. This is not a DIY SaaS product where they figure it out themselves. It is a ready-to-use system, handed over.

**Not a childcare product wearing a school costume.** Every assumption in the platform is built around academic microschools: multi-step enrollment, parent-partnership models, flexible programs, small teams, close relationships, and founders doing everything themselves.

---

## Business Model

Customers pay a monthly or annual subscription for their Stack tier. Annual plans include two months free, which improves lifetime value and reduces seasonal churn.

On top of the subscription, schools commission Custom Stack features — bespoke additions scoped, priced, and built specifically for their school. This is the highest-margin tier and the strongest competitive moat: a school that has custom workflows built for them is deeply embedded and unlikely to switch.

The relationship is ongoing. As a school grows, they upgrade their Stack. As their needs get more specific, they commission more custom work. Concierge onboarding is included for the first 10 customers and available as a premium add-on beyond that.

---

## Go-to-Market Strategy

### Phase 1 — Foundation (Months 1–3)

- Finalize the Sage Field case study with specific metrics: enrollment applications processed, revenue collected, hours saved per week, parent satisfaction
- Launch the School Stack website with the interactive product demo (tabbed preview, founder video, feature comparison vs. SchoolCues and Spark)
- Form School Stack LLC (separate from Sage Field LLC)
- Apply to the National Microschooling Center preferred provider directory — Spark is already listed; this is table stakes
- Close the first 3 paying customers via personal demo before any paid advertising

### Phase 2 — Community-Led Acquisition (Months 2–6)

Microschool founders concentrate in specific, highly accessible communities. Zero-cost acquisition:

- Facebook groups: Microschool Revolution, "Microschool Founders," state-specific homeschool groups (Texas alone has tens of thousands of members), NMC member community
- Approach: add value for weeks before ever mentioning the product. Answer operational questions. Share free resources. When someone posts about software pain, respond with a genuine answer and offer to show how you solved it at Sage Field.
- Lead magnets: free downloadable templates (enrollment agreement, liability waiver, parent handbook, health form) — all already built for Sage Field, branded School Stack, gated behind an email

### Phase 3 — Content & SEO (Months 3–9)

High-priority content targeting low-competition, high-intent keywords:

- "How to start a microschool in Texas"
- "Best microschool software 2025–2026"
- "SchoolCues alternatives"
- "How to manage microschool enrollment"
- "Microschool parent communication software"
- "How to collect tuition for a microschool"
- "Microschool application form template"

### Phase 4 — Partnerships (Months 4–12)

- **KaiPod Catalyst** — trains new microschool founders; be the recommended software at graduation
- **Microschool Revolution** — partner with their founder directory
- **National Microschooling Center** — preferred provider status + member pricing
- **Texas homeschool associations (THSC)** — sponsor events, exhibit at conferences, offer member pricing
- **Outschool, Prenda, and other microschool networks** — single partnership converts at scale

### Phase 5 — Direct Outreach (Ongoing)

The highest-converting channel for school software is direct, personal outreach from the founder. The story — "I built this for my own school, it worked, want to see it?" — closes faster than any ad.

Outreach sequence:

1. Find founders via Facebook groups, Instagram, NMC directory, Microschool Revolution listings
2. Engage with their content authentically for 1–2 weeks
3. DM or email: "I built the software for my own microschool, Sage Field — grew from 0 to 30 students with it. Happy to show you what it does in 15 minutes. No pitch, just a demo."
4. Demo → free trial → close

Microschool founders are solo operators making fast decisions. Unlike K-12 districts (6–12 month sales cycles), a well-positioned microschool deal can close in days.

---

## 90-Day Launch Checklist

- [ ] Form School Stack LLC in Texas (separate from Sage Field LLC)
- [ ] Secure schoolstack.com or equivalent domain
- [ ] Document Sage Field case study with specific metrics
- [ ] Build School Stack marketing website with interactive product demo and case study page
- [ ] Apply to NMC preferred provider directory
- [ ] Join 10+ microschool/homeschool Facebook groups; begin value-first engagement
- [ ] Publish first 3 SEO-targeted blog posts
- [ ] Create lead magnet package (enrollment agreement template + parent handbook + health form)
- [ ] Set up analytics (PostHog or equivalent) on the School Stack site
- [ ] Finalize pricing tiers and build Stripe billing for School Stack subscriptions
- [ ] Finalize annual plan pricing (2 months free) and 14-day free trial setup
- [ ] Reach out to 20 microschool founders with personal demo offer
- [ ] Close first 3 paying customers before any paid advertising
- [ ] Attend or present at one Texas homeschool/alternative education event
- [ ] Reach out to KaiPod Catalyst about partnership

---

## Proven at Sage Field

School Stack is not a pitch deck. It is running right now at Sage Field Microschool in Texas — managing real enrollment applications, real families, real payments, real teacher assignments, and real school operations. The platform has been through the full enrollment cycle, handled Stripe payments, managed waitlists, sent emails, tracked leads, and powered a parent and teacher portal used by real people.

That's the foundation every new customer is buying — not a prototype, not a beta, but a working system that was pressure-tested before it was ever sold.

---

## The Thesis

The microschool software market has a clear gap: there is no platform that provides operational completeness — from marketing and lead capture through enrollment, contracts, billing, parent communication, and admin operations — at a price and with a UX that small school founders can actually adopt.

SchoolCues is cheap but shallow. Spark is microschool-native but limited. Brightwheel is excellent but built for childcare. Omella handles payments but nothing else.

School Stack has already built what does not yet exist in this market. The go-to-market strategy is not about building — it is about distribution, community, and story. The founder-led story, the Sage Field proof case, the depth of the platform, and the specificity of the target market create a competitive position that well-funded competitors cannot easily replicate.

The goal is to be the first name every microschool founder hears when they ask "what software should I use to run my school?" — and the last one they ever need to switch away from.

- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, use Config tool.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- simplify: Review changed code for reuse, quality, and efficiency, then fix any issues found.
- less-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled remote agents (triggers) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring remote agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/triggers.
- claude-api: Build, debug, and optimize Claude API / Anthropic SDK apps. Apps built with this skill should include prompt caching. Also handles migrating existing Claude API code between Claude model versions (4.5 → 4.6, 4.6 → 4.7, retired-model replacements).
TRIGGER when: code imports `anthropic`/`@anthropic-ai/sdk`; user asks for the Claude API, Anthropic SDK, or Managed Agents; user adds/modifies/tunes a Claude feature (caching, thinking, compaction, tool use, batch, files, citations, memory) or model (Opus/Sonnet/Haiku) in a file; questions about prompt caching / cache hit rate in an Anthropic SDK project.
SKIP: file imports `openai`/other-provider SDK, filename like `*-openai.py`/`*-generic.py`, provider-neutral code, general programming/ML.
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

content.text: Good questions — let me check what icon libraries are already in the project before planning, and I'll give you my take on the logo question.

**On the logo question:** Yes, it's legal. Using brand logos to identify competitors you replace is protected under nominative fair use — this is standard practice on SaaS landing pages (Linear, Notion, etc. all do it). The diagram's framing ("these tools → School Stack") makes the comparative intent clear and doesn't imply endorsement. Just keep the logos unaltered.

**On implementation:** No icon library is installed. I'll add `simple-icons` (provides inline SVG paths — no CDN dependency, no external requests). Let me implement both changes.
